response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development across multiple languages and domains, including C++ during their tenure at National Instruments. However, their recent experience is heavily biased toward web and mobile development (Node.js, TypeScript, React) with limited focus on C++ or industrial automation. Security expertise (IEC 62443) is not evident, and cloud platform solutions are lacking. Their background suggests versatility but not deep specialization for the role's core requirements.
strengths: Strong C++ experience from National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI components)
Demonstrated ability to work on performance-critical systems (low-latency apps, real-time metadata handling)
Proven track record of architectural contributions and open-source project leadership (GISCollective)
concerns: Limited recent C++ development (last C++ role was 2015)
No visible experience with IEC 62443 or secure development lifecycle practices
Minimal exposure to industrial automation protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA) or cloud platforms (Azure)
